threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot newcomers by how they still think the bus is the fastest way to get anywhere; seasoned locals know it's all about timing the Overground.
Caught a guy on the 333 singing R&B to an empty seat like it was his personal stage. Only in Brixton, I swear.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw the neighbor’s cat perched on the bus stop sign, oblivious to the chaos around it. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab stand open at 3 AM selling vegan döner that actually tastes like the real deal. It's a late-night miracle!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can spot a tourist by their baffled expression at BART delays—it's like recognizing an old friend after a long absence.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a rider serenading the Muni with a saxophone while stuck in traffic. Only in the TL would you find music amidst the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all love the loud music at the Festa Major, but locals often just want a quiet evening on the balcony.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still look amazed at the Sagrada Família's façade, while locals just rush past, phone glued to their ear.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You spot someone in Imbibe asking for a flat white and look confused when they say it's not a café. Real locals know it's all about the takeout coffee.
Heard a group on the 59 arguing about whether Sainsbury's or Tesco has better meal deals. They didn’t notice the bus was moving until we hit a pothole.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw the old guy dancing with his parrot on a leash at Reuterplatz. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those random U-Bahn stations that feel more like art galleries? I saw a full-on yarn bombing at Bernaustrasse last week. Just another day in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate BART delays like it's a game. Tourists just stand there, puzzled, while we strategize our next move.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 31 laughing as he rode past the Civic Center, said he missed the days when the bus was 'less of a circus.' Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume Gràcia's plazas are just for sipping café. Locals know they’re stages for spontaneous concerts and pop-up markets where the real life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell when they're still using Google Maps to walk to places in Gràcia; locals just glide through the narrow streets without a second thought.
